title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m
raw_response: {"rating": 4.2, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is an opinion/open-letter. It consistently advances a right-leaning perspective by praising Donald Trump and U.S./Israeli military action, criticizing Obama and “left-leaning media,” and endorsing restrictive visa policies as strategically justified. The direction and consistency of these positions support a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author explicitly urges Americans to support Donald Trump and credits him with decisive, beneficial action, a clear alignment with right-leaning U.S. politics; (2) The letter endorses aggressive military intervention and regime change by force, portraying airstrikes as liberating and precise—hallmarks of a hawkish, right-leaning foreign policy stance; (3) It contrasts Trump favorably with Obama, faulting Obama for siding with the regime over protesters, reinforcing a partisan right-leaning narrative; (4) The author attacks “left-leaning media” as susceptible to regime propaganda and as a pressure source to end the war, a common right-leaning critique of mainstream media; (5) It supports Trump-era visa restrictions on Iranians as a necessary tool against the regime despite personal hardship, aligning with stricter immigration/security policies favored on the right; (6) The piece valorizes traditional monarchy and unity around Reza Pahlavi, reflecting a traditionalist, conservative orientation; (7) The framing is one-sided and absolutist (e.g., depicting the regime in purely evil terms and minimizing potential downsides of strikes), with no balancing counterarguments, reinforcing the consistent rightward tilt."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is an opinion/open-letter. It consistently advances a right-leaning perspective by praising Donald Trump and U.S./Israeli military action, criticizing Obama and “left-leaning media,” and endorsing restrictive visa policies as strategically justified. The direction and consistency of these positions support a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author explicitly urges Americans to support Donald Trump and credits him with decisive, beneficial action, a clear alignment with right-leaning U.S. politics; (2) The letter endorses aggressive military intervention and regime change by force, portraying airstrikes as liberating and precise—hallmarks of a hawkish, right-leaning foreign policy stance; (3) It contrasts Trump favorably with Obama, faulting Obama for siding with the regime over protesters, reinforcing a partisan right-leaning narrative; (4) The author attacks “left-leaning media” as susceptible to regime propaganda and as a pressure source to end the war, a common right-leaning critique of mainstream media; (5) It supports Trump-era visa restrictions on Iranians as a necessary tool against the regime despite personal hardship, aligning with stricter immigration/security policies favored on the right; (6) The piece valorizes traditional monarchy and unity around Reza Pahlavi, reflecting a traditionalist, conservative orientation; (7) The framing is one-sided and absolutist (e.g., depicting the regime in purely evil terms and minimizing potential downsides of strikes), with no balancing counterarguments, reinforcing the consistent rightward tilt.

explanation: This is an opinion/open-letter. It consistently advances a right-leaning perspective by praising Donald Trump and U.S./Israeli military action, criticizing Obama and “left-leaning media,” and endorsing restrictive visa policies as strategically justified. The direction and consistency of these positions support a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author explicitly urges Americans to support Donald Trump and credits him with decisive, beneficial action, a clear alignment with right-leaning U.S. politics; (2) The letter endorses aggressive military intervention and regime change by force, portraying airstrikes as liberating and precise—hallmarks of a hawkish, right-leaning foreign policy stance; (3) It contrasts Trump favorably with Obama, faulting Obama for siding with the regime over protesters, reinforcing a partisan right-leaning narrative; (4) The author attacks “left-leaning media” as susceptible to regime propaganda and as a pressure source to end the war, a common right-leaning critique of mainstream media; (5) It supports Trump-era visa restrictions on Iranians as a necessary tool against the regime despite personal hardship, aligning with stricter immigration/security policies favored on the right; (6) The piece valorizes traditional monarchy and unity around Reza Pahlavi, reflecting a traditionalist, conservative orientation; (7) The framing is one-sided and absolutist (e.g., depicting the regime in purely evil terms and minimizing potential downsides of strikes), with no balancing counterarguments, reinforcing the consistent rightward tilt.
